steps.title: Use the constant term property
steps.content: When multiplying $(2x - 3)$ and $(x + k)$, the constant term of the product equals the product of their constant terms. Since $2x^2 + 5x - 12$ has constant term $-12$: $$(-3) \cdot k = -12$$

steps.title: Solve for k and verify
steps.content:
Dividing both sides by $-3$: $$k = \frac{-12}{-3} = 4$$
Verify by expanding $(2x - 3)(x + 4)$: $$2x^2 + 8x - 3x - 12 = 2x^2 + 5x - 12$$ ✓
